MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Kaipo Young (kyoung@bl-plaw.com) of Malcom Leader-Picone for Judgment Creditor.
Herman Franck (916-447-8400; franckhermanlaw88@yahoo.com) of Franck & Associates, self-represented defendant/judgment debtor.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Herman Franck's Motion For Equitable Stay Of Enforcement Of Judgment Due To Covid-19 Pandemic:

Defendant Herman Franck's motion for equitable stay of enforcement of judgment is denied. On August 4, 2004, an amended modified judgment was entered on a jury verdict against Defendant Franck in the amount of $375,000 jointly and severally with Stephen T. Gargaro, plus $50,000 in punitive damages. On February 7, 2012, that judgment was renewed in the total amount of $551,471.32, taking into account costs, credits, and interest after judgment. On August 5, 2020, the judgment was again renewed, in the amount of $535,881.08. Defendant makes no attempt to explain or justify his more than 16-year delay in satisfying the underlying judgment, nor does he cite any colorable legal grounds or provide any compelling factual basis for granting him the extraordinary relief he seeks.

Counsel for the plaintiff/judgment creditor is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
